behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure lives entirely within the extraction/parsing algorithm. A transitive-dependency manifest format (e.g., package-lock.json, poetry.lock) already encodes the full graph in one file; the bug is whether the parser walks it correctly. No module handoff is required. REAL-INFRA: The function can accept manifest content as a string — no real filesystem or network needed. MOCK-HIDE: No dependency to hide. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection: Integration would be needed only if transitive resolution required calling out to a registry API; nothing in the behavior description implies that.
behaviors.test_description: Given manifest content (e.g., a package-lock.json string) containing two direct dependencies each with at least one transitive dependency, call the dependency-extraction function directly and assert that the returned collection contains all four package@version entries — both direct and transitive — with no omissions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is a module-boundary bug — the query-construction layer strips or ignores the version constraint before sending it to the HTTP client. This only manifests when the manifest-parser output is wired to the HTTP request builder. A single-function unit test on the query builder alone would not catch a missing glue step. REAL-INFRA: A mock HTTP server (e.g., httpretty or respx) can record the outgoing request and assert it contains the version range parameter — no real vulnerability database needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking at the scanner's own result layer would hide whether version ranges were ever included in the outgoing request. The mock must sit at the HTTP boundary. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the manifest parser to the HTTP query client using a mock HTTP server. Load a manifest with a package pinned to a specific version range (e.g., 'requests>=2.25.0,<2.28.0'). Invoke the vulnerability-check flow and capture the outgoing HTTP request. Assert that the request URL or body contains the version range constraint, not merely the package name.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is a pure sorting/comparison algorithm on in-memory data. The failure mode — wrong ordering — lives entirely within a single comparator or sort key function. No module handoff is involved. REAL-INFRA: No I/O. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection: Integration is not warranted because the behavior's own failure does not require any module handoff; the data is already an in-memory list of CVE records.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a list of CVE objects covering all four severity levels, with at least two CVEs sharing the same severity but different CVSS scores. Call the sort function directly and assert: (a) severity order is Critical > High > Medium > Low, and (b) within equal severity, lower CVSS score sorts after higher CVSS score.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure — mutating sibling entries — only appears when the parser-then-writer pipeline is exercised end-to-end on a real file representation. A pure in-memory string-transform unit test might not surface formatting-related side effects (e.g., lost comments, reordered keys, whitespace destruction) that cause 'other entries unchanged' to be violated. The bug lives at the boundary between the parser's data model and the serializer's output. REAL-INFRA: A temp file on disk is sufficient; no network needed. MOCK-HIDE: No external API. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Write a multi-dependency manifest to a temp file. Invoke the manifest-modification function targeting exactly one dependency's version. Re-read the temp file and assert: (a) the targeted dependency's version is the new value, and (b) every other dependency entry is byte-for-byte identical to the original (use a diff of the parsed entries).

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior explicitly requires 'the real repository.' REAL-INFRA: PR creation requires real HTTP to a hosted VCS API (GitHub/GitLab), real authentication, real branch creation, and real diff rendering. An in-process mock HTTP stub cannot verify that the diff is accepted as valid by the real API, that branch protections permit the operation, or that the PR appears with the correct content. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the VCS client always passes — it cannot detect a wrong diff payload, a rejected auth token, or a malformed branch ref. These failures only surface against the real API. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No (single PR creation action). Rejection: Integration is too low because the real failure modes are properties of the VCS API response, not of local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real (or dedicated test) repository on the VCS platform, run the full scanner pipeline on a manifest with one known vulnerable dependency. Assert: (a) exactly one new PR is opened, (b) the PR diff modifies exactly one version string in the manifest, and (c) no other files or dependency entries appear in the diff.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Semver comparison is a pure deterministic algorithm on two version strings. The failure mode — misidentifying a major bump — lives entirely within the comparison function. REAL-INFRA: No I/O. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No. Rejection: Integration adds no value here; the failure cannot hide behind a module boundary because the function takes two strings and returns a boolean.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize the semver-comparison function with cases: (1.x.x → 2.0.0) → breaking=True, (1.2.3 → 1.3.0) → breaking=False, (1.2.3 → 1.2.4) → breaking=False, (0.9.0 → 1.0.0) → breaking=True. Assert the function returns the expected classification for each case.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the handoff between modules: the breaking-change detector must communicate its decision to the PR-creation gate, and the output formatter must receive and render the review flag. Neither module alone can expose this; the bug is in the wiring. REAL-INFRA: No real VCS API needed — the PR creator can be a stub that records whether it was called. MOCK-HIDE: The stub is used to assert the PR creator is NOT called, which is exactly the correctness property. This is safe because the stub's 'was called' assertion is the test predicate, not a return-value fabrication. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No (single scanner pass). Rejection: System is not needed because no real VCS API is required to confirm the skip; Integration with a PR-creator stub is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the scanner with a manifest whose only vulnerable dependency requires a major version bump. Inject a stub PR creator that records calls. Run the scanner end-to-end. Assert: (a) the stub PR creator's call count is zero, and (b) the scanner's returned report or stdout contains a review-required flag referencing the dependency.
